sources.claim: OpenTelemetry semantic conventions standardize operation/data attribute naming across telemetry signals; Stage 6 uses stable method/failure attributes instead of prose-only lessons.

OpenTelemetry CICD semantic conventions recommend low-cardinality error.type and no error.type for successful operations; Stage 6 records one bounded CLI-shape error class and leaves successful wrapper runs non-error.

SLSA provenance requirements include metadata useful for debugging/investigations such as timestamps and identifiers; Stage 6 binds the lesson to source artifacts, timestamps, and validation identifiers.
Google SRE postmortem practice emphasizes preventive action items to avoid recurrence; Stage 6 turns the suppressed failure into a wrapper and regression fixture.

synthesis: Promote suppressed CLI-shape failure into a durable method-reliability record, executable wrapper, and validator fixture so future runs use the proven --root/--zip contract rather than re-learning the same failure.
